label: Ask me anything… (textarea)
expected_behavior: Auto-resizes on input (height=scrollHeight). Enter (without Shift) calls sendMessage(): trims value, removes .welcome block, appends a user .message bubble, shows typing indicator, disables #sendBtn, then routes the prompt through window.callClaude (claude-proxy.js Cloud Function) or falls back to a direct api.anthropic.com call using sessionStorage 'nbd_joe_key'. Shift+Enter inserts a newline.
notes: Pass 1 already rowed this — re-confirmed handler. Model claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, max_tokens 1024.

label: ➤ (send)
expected_behavior: Click invokes sendMessage() (same path as Enter). Disabled while a request is in flight (sendBtn.disabled=true) and re-enabled in the finally branch. On missing/invalid key in the fallback path, posts an inline AI bubble telling the user to add their Anthropic key in CRM Dashboard → Settings → Ask Joe AI.
notes: danger=send: dispatches an outbound LLM/API call (incurs token cost). Pass 1 rowed this.

label: ← Back to Dashboard
expected_behavior: Plain anchor navigation to /pro/dashboard.html. No JS handler.
notes: Pass 1 rowed this.

label: (no Scenarios button)
expected_behavior: FINDING: The standalone ask-joe.html has NO Scenarios button. The full Scenarios / New-Chat / API-key-activate control set the critic expected lives ONLY in the SPA #joe view (dashboard.html tpl-view-joe). The standalone page markup is just welcome block + textarea + send + back link (docs/pro/ask-joe.html lines 283-303). Key activation on the standalone page is implicit (sessionStorage 'nbd_joe_key' set elsewhere); there is no in-page Activate input.
notes: Documenting the gap as a non-existence finding. Rows gap-misc-005..012 capture the controls on the SPA #joe view they were confused with.

label: ⚡ Scenarios
expected_behavior: Delegated data-action='call' → window.openDecisionPicker(): if window.DecisionEngine.openPicker exists, opens the Decision Engine scenario picker (drops a battle-tested prompt into Ask Joe); else showToast('Decision engine loading...', 'error').
notes: This is the 'scenario buttons' control the gap referenced — singular Scenarios launcher, not per-scenario buttons inline. The per-scenario list is rendered by DecisionEngine.openPicker (out of scope here).

label: 🗑️ New Chat
expected_behavior: Delegated → clearJoeChat(): removes sessionStorage JOE_CHAT_STORE, resets _joeMessages=[], re-runs initJoeChat() to repaint the empty chat, then showToast('Chat cleared','success').
notes: Clears session chat only; does not clear the API key.

label: sk-ant-api03-… (API key input)
expected_behavior: Password-masked text input inside #joeNoKey. Holds the Anthropic key to be saved by the adjacent Activate button. Not persisted on its own.
reveal_state: after:no-key (shown only when getJoeKey() is empty — #joeNoKey display:none until then)
notes: reveal_state is conditional on no-key state set by initJoeChat (ai.js).

label: Activate
expected_behavior: Delegated → saveJoeKey(): reads #joeKeyInput, rejects with error toast if it does not start with 'sk-ant', else writes sessionStorage 'nbd_joe_key' (and removes any legacy localStorage copy), updates #joeKeyStatus, clears the input, re-runs initJoeChat() to swap from no-key to chat UI, and showToast('✓ Joe AI activated','success').
notes: The 'API-key activate' control the gap referenced. Standalone ask-joe.html has NO equivalent (see gap-misc-004).

label: 📋 Today's priorities / 🎯 Best leads to close / 📄 Supplement request / 💬 Adjuster pushback / 📊 Pipeline summary
expected_behavior: Each delegated → joeQuick(data-arg): sets #joeInput.value to the canned prompt then immediately calls sendJoeMessage() (fires a Claude request with live pipeline context).
reveal_state: after:has-key (inside #joeInputArea, shown once a key is present)
notes: danger=send: each triggers an outbound LLM call (token cost). Five distinct data-arg prompts; tested as one row group.

label: Ask Joe anything… (textarea)
expected_behavior: Chat composer. Enter handling is wired in dashboard-ui.js:636 (calls window.sendJoeMessage on Enter). sendJoeMessage (ai.js:286) builds rich Firestore context (buildJoeContext) and calls Claude.

label: SEND
expected_behavior: Delegated → sendJoeMessage() (ai.js:286): reads #joeInput, appends user bubble, builds live pipeline context, dispatches a Claude Haiku request, renders the response into #joeMessages, persists to JOE_CHAT_STORE.
notes: danger=send: outbound LLM call.

label: Change Key
expected_behavior: Delegated → clearJoeKey(): removes sessionStorage/localStorage 'nbd_joe_key' AND JOE_CHAT_STORE, resets _joeMessages=[], re-runs initJoeChat() so the no-key Activate UI (gap-misc-007/008) reappears.
notes: Also wipes chat history as a side effect (clears JOE_CHAT_STORE).

label: Today / 7 Days / 30 Days / All Time
expected_behavior: Delegated (DOMContentLoaded querySelectorAll('[data-range]')) → window.setRange(range, btn): sets module `range`, moves the .active class to the clicked tab, then render() re-buckets allDocs by getRangeStart(range) and repaints stat cards, chart, feature table, model table, and cost breakdown. 'all' uses epoch 2020-01-01.
notes: Four time-filter tabs. Pass 1 likely captured 3 of the 4 (or the group as one) — this row covers all 4 as the canonical interactive filter set. Projected-cost meta recomputes per range.

label: ↻ Refresh
expected_behavior: Delegated → window.loadData(): sets button to '⏳ Loading…' + disabled, queries Firestore api_usage (where uid==current user, orderBy timestamp desc), rebuilds allDocs, render(), stamps #lastRefresh, showToast('✓ Analytics refreshed · N records'). On index error → 'Firestore index needed' toast; on other error → renderEmpty + error toast. Auto-fires every 60s via bootAnalytics setInterval.
notes: Read-only Firestore read; danger=none.

label: (chart bars — hover only)
expected_behavior: FINDING: chart bars have NO click drilldown. They are decorative: CSS hover brightens them (.chart-bar:hover) and each carries a title='N requests · LABEL' tooltip. renderChart() builds them via innerHTML with no addEventListener and no data-action. There is no drilldown-by-bucket interaction to test.
notes: Documents the absence of chart drilldowns the critic asked about — hover tooltip only.

label: (no export / download / CSV control)
expected_behavior: FINDING: analytics.html has NO export controls. The only interactive controls on the page are the 4 time tabs (gap-misc-013), the Refresh button (gap-misc-014), and the topbar nav links (plain anchors, covered by the global nav inventory). The feature/model tables and cost breakdown are render-only (no row clicks, no CSV/download). Confirmed by full read of analytics.html and pro-analytics.js.
notes: Closes out the 'export controls beyond the 3 rows' gap as a non-existence finding. Pass 1's 3 rows ≈ the time-tab group + refresh; nothing further exists.

label: Undo (or 'Retry' depending on caller)
expected_behavior: Rendered by showToast() ONLY when the caller passes an undoAction. Delegated click (ui.js:1137 data-ui-action map) → window._toastUndo(toastId) → looks up the stored callback in activeToasts and invokes it, then closes the toast. Live callers: crm-pipeline.js stage-move failure (undoText='Retry', re-runs moveCard) and lead-type-change failure (undoText='Retry', re-runs changeLeadType).
notes: The generic clickable toast Undo affordance. In the current codebase its only wired callers are the two crm-pipeline retry toasts (label='Retry'). The default label is 'Undo' for any future caller.

label: ✕ (toast close)
expected_behavior: Always present on every toast. Delegated → window._closeToast(toastId): fades + slides the toast out and deletes its activeToasts entry. Dismisses without running any undoAction.
notes: Captured because the gap asked for toast affordances; the ✕ is the universal one (the Undo at gap-misc-017 is conditional).

label: ✕ (remove widget)
expected_behavior: Delegated (data-w-action) → NBDWidgets.removeWidget(id): drops the id from the active-widgets list, saves, re-renders the widget home, and showToast('Widget removed','info'). FINDING: this toast is fired WITHOUT an undoAction, so there is NO clickable Undo on widget-remove (contrary to the critic's flag) — removal is immediate and only reversible via the widget picker / resetDefaults.
notes: danger=delete (removes a configured widget). Documents that the expected Undo affordance is absent on this path — a real gap to flag, not just inventory.

route: N/A (snooze via kanban context-menu / customer banner)
selector: LeadSnooze.snooze() toast (no selector — no undo button rendered)
label: (no Undo on snooze toast)
expected_behavior: FINDING: LeadSnooze.snooze()/unsnooze() update Firestore and re-render, but their success toasts are plain showToast(msg,type) calls with NO undoAction — so there is NO clickable Undo on snooze either. To reverse a snooze the rep uses the customer-page 'Wake up now / Unsnooze' banner button or the kanban ⏰ Unsnooze context-menu item (separate controls, owned by other inventory areas). No transient Undo toast exists.
notes: Closes the 'clickable Undo on snooze' part of the gap as a non-existence finding. Undo here is a persistent banner/menu action, not a toast.

route: modal:nbd-dedup-overlay (fires from Add/Edit Lead save when duplicates found)
selector: button.nbd-dedup-match-btn[data-lead-id] (one per matched lead, up to 3)
label: <existing lead name> · High match / Possible · Click to open
expected_behavior: Per-match row inside #nbd-dedup-matches. Click closes the overlay and resolves promptUser with {action:'open', leadId} → checkAndPrompt returns {proceed:false, openLeadId} so the caller ABORTS the new save and navigates to the existing lead instead. Hover swaps the row background. Built by LeadDedup.promptUser from findDuplicates (high=same phone/address, medium=same name+street).
reveal_state: modal:nbd-dedup-overlay (only when ≥1 duplicate match on new-lead save)
notes: Up to 3 match rows (matches.slice(0,3)). Choosing one discards the in-progress new lead — a save-divergence path, hence P1.

label: Cancel
expected_behavior: Closes the overlay and resolves {action:'cancel'} → checkAndPrompt returns {proceed:false} so the new-lead save is ABORTED entirely (lead is NOT created, and no existing lead is opened). Clicking the dimmed backdrop (e.target===overlay) does the same.
notes: Backdrop click is an equivalent cancel affordance (same resolve). P1 because it gates whether the lead persists.

label: Create anyway
expected_behavior: Closes the overlay and resolves {action:'create', duplicateOf: matches[0].lead.id} → checkAndPrompt returns {proceed:true, duplicateOf} so the caller SAVES the new lead anyway, stamping a duplicateOf audit reference to the top match. Use for genuinely separate households at a shared address/phone.
notes: danger left 'none' (creates a lead, not a destructive/send/charge action) but P1 — it is the override that lets a duplicate persist; verify the duplicateOf stamp lands.
